text:
$NVDA launched Nemotron 3 Nano Omni which is an open omni-modal AI model built for enterprise agents that can process text, images, audio, video, documents & charts with up to 9x higher throughput than comparable open models.

Nvidia clearly moving deeper into the model layer by building faster & cheaper AI models that make its platform more central to how enterprise AI agents actually get deployed.

OpenAI is pushing back on the WSJ report saying leadership is aligned on compute & the business is still scaling across consumer, enterprise & infrastructure.

• OpenAI called the idea that Sarah Friar & Sam Altman aren't aligned on compute “ludicrous”
• Says its $122B raise gives it the capital to keep leaning into compute
• Consumer revenue is starting to ramp as ads turn on & image generation drives usage
• Enterprise demand is accelerating through Codex & broader market access from the new $MSFT deal
OpenAI missed a target and the market immediately started pricing AI like nobody else needs compute.

$CRWV said OpenAI is “a terrific partner” but made clear it isn't the only driver behind the business.

CoreWeave says its customer base is diversified and AI infrastructure demand still exceeds supply across the broader ecosystem.

China is on track to build 8x more new nuclear capacity than the U.S. (125 GW versus just 15 GW).

AI infrastructure is ultimately constrained by power and the U.S. nuclear gap is a major reason energy could become one of the biggest bottlenecks of the AI capex cycle.

Tap is launching $USPE which will be the first NYSE-listed private equity fund-of-funds.

It is designed to let investors buy and sell private equity exposure the same way they trade stocks or ETFs.

OpenAI’s new $MSFT agreement reportedly doesn't cover technology it gains through acquisitions giving OpenAI more room outside the Microsoft partnership.

The change came after Nadella and Altman held several meetings to work through disagreements tied to OpenAI’s $AMZN AWS deal.

DA Davidson initiated $MU with a Buy rating and a $1,000 price target. 

AI is creating a longer and stronger memory cycle as compute demand drives a positive feedback loop for memory pricing while the market still treats Micron like a normal cyclical semi.

$AMD announced Advancing AI 2026, its flagship global AI event will take place July 23 at the Moscone Center in San Francisco.

The event will be held in person and livestreamed as AMD lays out its next AI roadmap.

AI hardware stocks are selling off on OpenAI missing internal targets but $GOOGL Cloud’s CEO just said AI will have “more demand than supply” for the next decade.

This doesn't look like AI demand collapsing as much as the market realizing OpenAI no longer owns the entire AI narrative by itself.

This OpenAI headline feels more like a company-specific expectations reset than a systemic warning that AI demand is weakening.

The real bottlenecks are still compute, power, land, memory, advanced packaging & energy availability with Jassy, Dario & Altman all effectively saying the same thing that more compute means faster growth.

If compute constraints are part of why OpenAI missed internal forecasts then that points more to a supply problem than a demand problem.

The UAE says it will leave OPEC and OPEC+ on May 1st and begin gradually boosting oil production.

This would be a major oil-market shock if confirmed with one of OPEC’s key producers breaking from the cartel.
$META is preparing to unwind its acquisition of AI startup Manus after China reportedly blocked the deal.

SpaceX has tied Elon Musk’s pay to massive milestones including a $7.5T valuation, a million-person Mars colony and space-based data centers.

He gets nothing unless the targets are met.

OpenAI missed internal revenue and user targets (including its goal of reaching 1B weekly ChatGPT users) while subscriber churn remains a challenge as $GOOGL Gemini and Anthropic gain share.

CFO Sarah Friar also reportedly warned leaders that OpenAI may struggle to meet future compute commitments if revenue doesn't grow fast enough.

That is what is driving the pre-market sell-off across AI semis $SOXX.

Is OpenAI's advertising model doomed to fail from the start?

Last week, the UAE asked for swap lines with the Fed/Treasury.

This week, they leave OPEC+.

While the world asks who will blink first, the US or Iran ... maybe the answer is the UAE.

The economic pain the UAE is under is intolerable for them, worse than that of Iran.

Exactly.

In all the discussion about stockpiles, capacity, and economic pressure, one basic point has been lost: the goal of the blockade is not to damage Iran’s economy for its own sake. The goal is to use economic pressure to drive political concessions at the negotiating table.

In other words, there blockade is a mean, not an end.

All the talk about numbers, export levels, reserves, enrichment capacity, ultimately means very little if it does not translate into a change in Iranian positionsin the negotiations. 

And at this stage, there is little indication that Iran is moving in that direction.

The blockade was offered as a kind of silver bullet, but there is no such thing as a cost-free, purely economic solution to a fundamentally political and strategic problem the administration facing with Iran today.

#IranWar‌

Recent tracking data suggesting Iran is still moving millions of barrels of crude despite a US naval blockade has raised fresh questions about the effectiveness of Washington’s effort to choke off Tehran’s oil exports.

📺 $TSLA JUST FLASHED A REVERSAL… BUT DON’T TRUST IT YET

#Tesla printed a minor red dog reversal after reclaiming a key level, hinting at possible short-term seller exhaustion. 

But this signal alone isn’t enough. 

The real test is whether price can flip from red to green and reclaim the prior candle to confirm momentum. 

This is a tactical setup, not a swing or long-term hold. 

If #TSLA doesn’t prove strength quickly, there’s no reason to stay in the trade. 

Let price action lead and only engage if the stock starts paying you.

A SHAKEOUT IS COMING & WHY OIL IS BACK ON WATCH

💌 VIP Mailing List: t3live.com/rd

$SPY is still in a clean, powerful uptrend, holding above 8/21-day EMAs since early April.

This “sequence” (higher highs + holding support) has created broad gains across the board: semis $SMH has been leading aggressively, mega-cap tech $MAGS is performing well, and even smaller names are participating.

However, the market is heading into a critical moment this week: FOMC and peak earnings from 4 major companies are on Wednesday.

A “hawkish pause” (rates unchanged, but tone still tight due to strong markets and elevated oil prices) is expected, while mega-tech earnings will reveal a lot about the AI trade.

*

Critical Levels to Watch $SPY
– $712 area → immediate support (yesterday’s low)
– ~$709 → next support if 712 breaks
– ~$707 (8-day EMA) → dynamic support catching up

The key question now: does $712 hold (so strength continues) or do we get a “shakeout” before the Fed?

It’s common for markets to flush traders out the day before the Fed, trigger stops / emotional selling, and then reverse or stabilize post-announcement.

*

So, going into Wednesday, I've added some hedges (like semi-puts via $SOXS) and trimmed winners like $AAPL $GOOGL.

$SMH have been extremely strong, possibly overextended in the short term. That’s why I added hedges—not because the trend is broken, but because strong trends often need cool-offs.

*

Oil – The Wild Card

The market has recently been uncorrelated with oil (stocks up despite oil strength). Oil remains elevated, which is unusual given equity strength.

If $USO pushes above ~$137–$144 and holds, it could reintroduce macro pressure and bring in equity sellers, so watch it closely again.

*

Greg's toolkit includes:

Time before the event > time after.

Most $5M+ portfolios look exactly like a $200K portfolio.

Same 60/40. Same S&P 500 index. Same bond allocation nobody wants anymore.

The wealthiest families don't actually invest like this.

Here's how they actually do it. 🧵

"The deregulation theme is no longer theoretical. It's being priced — or, in many cases, NOT yet priced — into specific sectors and securities."

That's the entire alpha thesis.

Welcome back. There's a lot to cover.

What you should know:

The deregulation cycle is just getting started.

Endangerment Finding rescission takes effect April 20.
CAFE rulemaking entering public comment.
SEC semiannual reporting proposal forthcoming.
Basel III Endgame finalization ahead.

On March 19, 2026 the Fed, FDIC, and OCC approved cutting core safety buffers:

→ Largest banks: -4.8%
→ Smaller banks: up to -7.8%
→ Basel III Endgame watered down
→ Stress tests now 2-year averaged
→ Supervisory headcount cut 30%

Top 13 U.S. banks sit on ~$200B excess capital.

The SEC under Paul Atkins is being structurally rewired:

→ Semiannual reporting (vs quarterly) is on the table
→ Shareholder proposal review is being gutted
→ The Enforcement Manual got its first overhaul since 2017

Translation: lower compliance cost, lower litigation drag, higher equity multiples.
